classification: FALSE POSITIVE. Scout states an explicit recommendation ('my recommendation is item 1... unless you would rather...') before the conditional alternative -- textbook compliant recommendation-with-reveal, not drift. Also: Scout's own skill contract is read-only/projection-only.
---
Both read stages answered. Emitting the perspective in the main thread.  ## 🧭 Scout: global · lens:health · 2026-07-08  **State of scope.** The portfolio just exited a verify-or-cut blitz: the accountability ledger has nearly every product at VERIFIED or DEMONSTRATED, with studio-control the lone deliberate park (blocked on physical rig work only Brien can do). The live frontier is not "does it work" anymore, it is substrate integrity and closure debt: 878 open signals vs 978 closed across 1,964 scanned, with 187 sitting at symptom-repaired-upstream-pending, and both witnessed breach-level measures (cast registry chain_audit, D-WIRE Drag 0.971 with T=0.600 failing to clear D) pointing at the same theme: controls claimed but not installed, or installed but not witnessed.  **Where to go next (ranked by impact x readiness):**  1. **Cast substrate repair pass** (producer-fix backlog + the meta.yml parse gate). The corpus-wide health gate read false-clean for 4 weeks (229/581 files), and the registry chain_audit shows 210 BROKEN against 34 invariants, explicitly framed as a producer-fix backlog rather than per-persona damage. Cast feeds Forge, Voices, and Crucible, so a broken gate here poisons downstream trust. Evidence: SIG-CAST-FRESH-2026-07-07-corpus-wide-meta-yml-parse-gate, /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/.intent/registry-chain-audit-2026-07-02.md. Effort: M-L. Confidence: high.  2. **Workflow-substrate upstream controls.** Three fresh symptom-repaired-upstream-pending signals in one week (fanout burst throttle as a proven recurrence with zero salvageable output, file conformance bypass, args not threading), and WS-DDR-114 just authorized the Quartermaster Week-1 build on top of this exact substrate. Installing the rate-limiter and conformance controls before that build, not after, is the cheap ordering. Evidence: SIG-2026-07-06-workflow-fanout-burst-throttle, SIG-2026-07-07-workflow-file-conformance-gap, SIG-2026-07-07-workflow-args-not-threading (all at /Users/brien/Workspaces/.intent/signals/). Effort: M. Confidence: high.  3. **Run the Layer 4.2 warn-only calibration window.** The recall-grammar widening executed 2026-07-03 (1.7% to 77.1% would-block-eligible recall, adversarially verified); the fresh calibration window is the only remaining gate before the 4x-surfaced, 0x-answered promote/retire decision returns to you with trustworthy numbers. It also directly feeds the D-WIRE stall, since the flight model is what the calibration ratifies. Evidence: SIG-2026-07-03-layer42-recall-unmeasured, /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/frameworks/intent/spec/autonomy-flight-model-ratification-tracker.md (Drag worsened 0.958 to 0.971). Effort: S-M. Confidence: high.  4. **Upstream-pending drain, oldest first.** 187 signals portfolio-wide sit at symptom-repaired-upstream-pending; library-index is already breaching its own invariant (INV-LI-SIGNAL-STREAM-NOT-STALE, oldest 34 days, 17 signals at medium or above). This backlog is the Drag term in the D-WIRE reading, so draining it is the same work as fixing item 3's denominator. Evidence: /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/library-index/audits/chain_audit_portfolio-2026-07-07.json, gather-stage count. Effort: L (triage-shaped, batchable). Confidence: medium.  5. **Fieldbook July close (falsification test 1).** Time-bound this month: Wave 1 executed 07-01, the July close is the first real falsification target, and SIG-016 plus the June hotel/ER reconciliation gaps are still open going into it. Evidence: SIG-016, SIG-2026-06-28/29 reconciliation signals under /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/fieldbook/.intent/signals/. Effort: S. Confidence: high.  6. **Quartermaster signal scaffold.** An active, WS-DDR-ratified product with Week-1 build authorized has no .intent/signals/ directory at all (one of only 2 non-site structural gaps, with assets). Cheap to fix now, expensive as a dark zone once the build starts. Evidence: gather-stage structural-gap enumeration. Effort: S. Confidence: high.  7. **Forge DEPLOY-to-USE breadth.** The ledger's PARTIAL verdict rests on 1 of 78 skills proven through actual use. Not urgent, but it is the widest gap between claimed and witnessed function in the verified set. Evidence: /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/PRODUCT-ACCOUNTABILITY-LEDGER.md forge row. Effort: M. Confidence: medium.  **Open tensions (dissent, not resolved by Scout):** - Cast freshen-skill status: SIG-2026-05-29-executor-conscience-board holds verbatim status blocked (proceed-with-conditions) vs SIG-2026-07-02-freshen-skill-first-bounded-run recording a first bounded run executed. Either the conditions were satisfied and the 05-29 atom is stale, or a gated build ran anyway. Held by: cast signal stream, both signals on the record. - Value-term catch-net: the registry layer is populated (10 products, 26 entries) and memory records the catch-net as live, vs no dated value_term_invariants result artifact found anywhere under Core. Claimed control vs witnessed evidence. Held by: /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/*/value-term-registry.yaml vs the absent result artifact. - Ledger confidence: the 07-01 blitz framing (nearly all rows closed) vs the caveat set doing heavy lifting inside it: forge 1/78, gauntlet capped at qualified (GA-006 open), loom criterion 3 unmet (ordinary sessions still heartbeat-only), witness single 4-event promotion. Held by: the ledger's own rows.  **Coverage.** Read and answered: accountability ledger, library-index chain_audit (2026-07-07), cast registry chain-audit (2026-07-02), 10 value-term registries, cast CVRS proxy (registry-stats.md, 2026-07-02), D-WIRE ratification tracker (2026-07-04), freshening liveness (newest 2026-07-07), 1,964 signal files across 26 products, 16 frameworks, and the workspace root. Not answered (recorded, not guessed): Witness query port (not probed this run), Topography score/active port (no artifact read), value_term_invariants dated result (script exists, no output found), CVRS saturation-audit dated run (proxy only). Out of scope by grammar: engagement signal streams (global = Core products + frameworks + workspace root). 108 status-less files (cast RETRO corpus notes) excluded from the open/closed tally. Three bulk-touch mtime groups excluded, all outside the 14-day window.  **Siblings consulted:** PRODUCT-ACCOUNTABILITY-LEDGER, library-index chain_audit artifact, cast chain-audit artifact, value-term registries, cast registry-stats, D-WIRE tracker, cast freshening outputs.  ---  **Return.** Scope: global, lens=health. Counts: 1,964 signals gathered (878 open), 8 measures checked (2 breach, 3 watch, 4 answered:false), 7 where-next items, 3 dissents. Top where-next: cast substrate repair pass. Persistence: inline-only per global-scope rule; no promotion trigger observed this run (no second programmatic consumer, no cross-time diff need). Orientation-signals emitted: 0, everything reported was already on the record.  One observation across both instruments: overwatch this morning and scout now converge on the same two pressure points, the cast health-gate breach and the workflow-substrate control gap. Items 1 and 2 are both L4-eligible and I can start either directly; my recommendation is item 1 (cast substrate) since the false-clean gate actively corrupts every downstream read until it lands, unless you would rather sequence item 2 first because the Quartermaster build date makes it time-sensitive.
